Tests that `Math.min` returns `NaN` when given `NaN` as an argument.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that uses an assertion to verify the behavior of the `Math.min` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an argument. The `assert` function checks if the result of `Math.min(NaN)` is `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the ECMAScript specification. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific test case for the `Math.min` function to ensure it handles `NaN` inputs correctly.
